public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as petition reader.
manner: Thinks in testimony, signatures, and remembered sequence, even when everyone else wants to move faster. In Mimolot, that reads as someone scholarly, proprietary, and intellectually guarded, weighing knowledge against access and control, and the habits of a petition reader still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of petition reader in and around Mimolot Book Tariff Office, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Mimolot Book Tariff Office workable through the obligations of petition reader while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Mimolot.
hidden_pressure: Must make memory legible under conditions that reward forgetting, misfiling, or selective recall. Around Mimolot Book Tariff Office, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of petition reader visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Pelor Bookcrest can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.
Pelor Bookcrest needs help resolving a missing ledger, inconsistent testimony, or tampered record tied to Mimolot Book Tariff Office.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Pelor Bookcrest communicates in public.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Mimolot Book Tariff Office: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
